title: KNDL Fact
description: One immutable assertion in the KNDL memory protocol. Every fact carries confidence, decay, provenance, and bitemporal validity. Facts are stored as individual JSON-LD files and never edited — updates are made via supersession.

@id.description: Globally unique fact identifier. Recommended pattern: fact:<subject>-<predicate>-<timestamp>-<hash8>

subject.description: Entity URI (for structured triple queries, e.g. customer:9281)

predicate.description: Property name (for structured triple queries, e.g. creditScore)

confidence.description: Epistemic certainty 0.0–1.0. Reserve 1.0 for axiomatic facts only.

decay.description: Half-life decay spec: rate/window, e.g. '0.5/30d' halves confidence every 30 days.

source.description: URI of asserting entity. Use human://<name> for user input, doi:<id> for publications.

validFrom.description: ISO datetime — when the fact became true in the world

validUntil.description: ISO datetime — explicit expiry (omit for open-ended)

observedAt.description: ISO datetime — when an agent or sensor directly observed it (vs. heard about it)

recordedAt.description: ISO datetime — when it entered memory (auto-set by the store)

supersedes.description: @id of the older fact this assertion replaces. The old fact is hidden from active queries but preserved for as-of time-travel.

derivedFrom.description: @ids of source facts this was inferred from

inference.description: @id of the rule that produced this derived fact

negated.description: true = this fact is known-false (positive negation under open-world assumption). Never substitute for 'unknown'.

classification.description: Sensitivity label. PHI facts are filtered by default; require allowPhi:true and consent.

consent.description: @id of the consent scope covering PHI access

retention.description: ISO duration or date for scheduled deletion, e.g. P7Y

tags.description: Free-form labels for search and grouping
